authorization.scope: narrow B2 filesystem qualification, exhaustive hosted fault coverage, bounded process-kill evidence, and rootless power-cut evidence correction

discovered_gaps.qualification: The entry required an evidence-qualified filesystem and exact mount tuple but did not freeze a runtime-observable qualification key or fail-closed matching rule.
discovered_gaps.fallback: The entry and ADR made unqualified stores read-only while the public contract allowed PreferWritable fallback only for writer contention.
discovered_gaps.coverage: The accepted text required process and VM kills at every named transition, including read-only validation and duplicated before states that cannot test persistence ordering.
discovered_gaps.tooling: The trusted lane is frozen but absent, and the entry allowed paths omitted the xtask command help and command-audit owners that must describe it truthfully.

qualification_key.selection: Obtain the held root descriptor's statx mount ID and match exactly one record in /proc/self/mountinfo; normalize its comma-separated VFS and super-option sets and require the exact values above. Missing data, read or parse error, no match, multiple matches, any option difference, filesystem difference, or magic difference is unqualified.
qualification_key.probe_rule: A successful write, rename, sync, or capability probe never creates qualification; only exact membership in this frozen tuple does.

correction.open_semantics: ReadOnly always remains read-only. Opening an existing store with PreferWritable on an unqualified tuple succeeds only as an explicitly reported read-only session, just like writer contention. It performs no writer-only staging cleanup or mutation.
correction.new_destination_semantics: Create, SaveAs, first provisional Autosave, and every other operation that would install a new destination on an unqualified tuple fail with UnsupportedFilesystem before source reads, staging, or destination mutation.
correction.bypass: No production feature, configuration, environment variable, alternate path, or runtime probe may bypass qualification. A crate-private cfg(test)-only detector seam is permitted and must not exist in a release build or public API.
correction.hosted_injection: Every named transition in transition_inventory supports deterministic before and after injection plus zero-based selection of every repeated occurrence; manual and autosave ref lanes are distinct cases.
correction.process_kill: Fresh-process SIGKILL, reopen, and exact retry cover every transition occurrence that mutates filesystem or lease state or can leave writer-private residue. Pure reads, validation, expected-parent comparison, root scans, and candidate listing instead require deterministic fault injection plus byte-identical no-mutation evidence.
correction.vm_rule: The rootless power-cut harness cuts only distinct post-transition persistence or authority boundaries listed in vm_post_transition_boundaries, plus one pre-sequence baseline. An adjacent before edge representing the same on-disk state is not a second VM case. Manual/autosave lanes, different directories, and repeated mutations remain distinct when their recoverable states differ.
correction.hosted_only_transition_classes: Open, commit-prefix comparison, private stage creation/write/copy, staging cleanup, maintenance lease upgrade/restore, root scan, and candidate listing do not receive VM cuts; they retain hosted injection and process-kill coverage where the process-kill rule applies.

harness_limits.cross_device: the two independent ext4 filesystems must exercise positive copy-and-rehash Save As
harness_limits.power_loss: rootless nbdkit cache=writeback must discard guest-unpersisted writes while explicit guest flushes reach the backing image

correction.report: Bind commit/tree/clean state, fixture and executable digests, exact qualification tuple, filesystem features, filesystem/kernel/mount/QEMU/nbdkit facts, hosted/process/VM transition and kill matrices, byte/object/work counters, product display facts, source digests, performance facts, results, and every failure without private paths.
correction.claim_boundary: This correction freezes the qualification and evidence target. It does not claim that the runtime gate, trusted lane, hostile fixture, VM harness, performance measurements, or durability evidence exists or passes.

superseded_fields: wp10b-project-store-public-actor-lifecycle-correction.json non_goals power-loss/filesystem qualification and matching stop condition, only for this bounded B2 checkpoint
PROJECT_STORE_DURABILITY_BRIEF.md and ADR-0003 wording requiring process or VM kills at every read-only transition
invariants: The store remains EXPERIMENTAL, off-product through B3, and unreachable from the product.
No persisted byte, path, transition name, public export, command, completion, fault, limit, dependency, or product edge changes.
Unknown and unqualified filesystems never receive write authority, and no probe or test seam is a production bypass.
Hosted failure coverage remains exhaustive; VM evidence is limited only to states that can distinguish persistence or authority outcomes.
The existing package, VM memory/disk/time ceilings, zero-retry rule, performance thresholds, report facts, cross-device case, and no-large-dataset rule remain binding.
evidence: exact correction binding, normalized-contract check, and unchanged public-root/dependency/wire/limit guards
future exhaustive hosted failpoint and bounded fresh-process kill/reopen/retry matrices
future rootless power-cut and cross-device evidence on the exact qualified tuple
future hostile lifecycle, responsiveness, incremental-rewrite, metadata-RSS, and sanitized report evidence
non_goals: B3 application integration or autosave timer scheduling
B4 product wiring, predecessor deletion, product validation, or a product durability claim
qualification of another filesystem, mount tuple, kernel, platform, network store, FUSE, or overlay filesystem
large microscopy data, TiB images, 4K, paid CI, or a public self-hosted runner
stop_condition: Any persisted byte/path, transition name, public API or dependency must change; the exact tuple cannot be recognized descriptor-relatively; nbdkit cannot discard unpersisted writes; distinct cross-device filesystems cannot be used; or the 900-second/640-MiB ceilings cannot be met.
rollback: revert this correction and its B2 qualification implementation together while the project-v15 product route remains sole
